lease_schema.generation: storage generation returned by GCS, tracked by the caller but not trusted inside the object body

compatibility_mapping.rationale: Existing runner logic already uses owner_id as the core holder identity.
Useful for operator visibility and debugging across machines.
Needed for liveness inspection and stale-lease analysis.
TTL-based fail-closed behavior already exists conceptually and should remain first-class.
Allows one holder to describe role-scoped subclaims without inventing a second coordination model.
Explicit machine_label improves cross-machine debugging and aligns with current runtime env conventions.
Needed to distinguish workstation, VM, and future workflow execution paths.
Supports forensic traceability between lease holder and code revision.
Supports future cloud-native audit packets and differentiated control handling.

acquire.success_condition: object did not previously exist and caller becomes the sole current holder
acquire.failure_condition: precondition failure if object already exists

renew.success_condition: caller still owns the current generation and extends heartbeat/expires_at
renew.failure_condition: precondition failure if another holder replaced or stole the lease

release.success_condition: lease removed only by the current holder
release.failure_condition: precondition failure if generation has changed

steal_after_expiry.mechanism: read current object, verify expiry, then rewrite with ifGenerationMatch=<expired_generation>
steal_after_expiry.success_condition: expired lease is replaced atomically by the new holder
steal_after_expiry.failure_condition: precondition failure if another actor renewed or stole first

validation_matrix: Acquire succeeds when no lease object exists.
Concurrent acquire attempts result in exactly one success and one precondition failure.
Renew succeeds for the current holder and bumps heartbeat/expires_at.
Renew fails when the caller uses a stale generation.
Release succeeds only for the current holder generation.
Release fails for a stale generation.
Steal after expiry succeeds only when the observed generation still matches the expired lease.
Steal after expiry fails if the original holder renewed before the replace.
Audit artifact is written for acquire, renew, release, and steal events.
Dry-run mode exercises the state machine without enabling broker-facing execution.
rollout_steps: Publish the lease contract and validation matrix.
Implement helper functions in the sanctioned runner code paths without enforcing them yet.
Add dry-run tests for contention, stale renewal, release mismatch, and expired steal.
Enable audit artifact emission for every lease transition.
Turn on enforcement only after dry-run validation is green on both workstation and VM paths.
